name: Kasakrtsna
dates_notes: Pre-Bādarāyaṇa; cited in Brahma-Sūtra 1.4.22; no work survives.
core_thesis: As reconstructed from later citations: Kāśakṛtsna survives only as the named source of the position recorded at Brahma-Sūtra 1.4.22 — *avasthiter iti kāśakṛtsnaḥ* ("the supreme self is established [in the *jīva* itself], says Kāśakṛtsna"). The doctrine attributed to him on the *jīva-Brahman* relation is the strongest of the three sūtra-cluster views: the supreme Brahman is itself present *as* the *jīva*; difference is only an apparent product of conditioning, while the underlying ontology is one of identity. Śaṅkara explicitly takes Kāśakṛtsna's *avasthitiḥ* to anticipate the Advaita non-difference reading and treats Bādarāyaṇa's silence after this third option as endorsement. Later Advaitins therefore read him as the canonical proto-Advaitin within the Brahma-Sūtra itself; Bhāskara and the *bhedābheda* schools read his position more weakly, as identity-with-distinction. Nothing else of the historical Kāśakṛtsna is recoverable; his name designates a single sūtra-locus and the strong-identity reading attached to it.

engaged_works.title: Positions reconstructed from Brahma-Sūtra citations
engaged_works.ascription_notes: No work survives; the proto-identity-thesis is reconstructed solely from Brahma-Sūtra 1.4.22.
engaged_works.summary: A single sūtra preserves Kāśakṛtsna: Brahma-Sūtra 1.4.22 (*avasthiter iti kāśakṛtsnaḥ*), the third position in the cluster on the *jīva*'s relation to Brahman that begins with Āśmarathya's *bhedābheda* (1.4.20) and Auḍulomi's *mokṣa*-state non-difference (1.4.21). Kāśakṛtsna's *avasthitiḥ* is glossed by Śaṅkara as the supreme Brahman's own being-present in the *jīva* itself — not a part, not a manifestation, not merely the *mokṣa*-state truth, but the standing condition. Śaṅkara reads Bādarāyaṇa's silence after this third option as decisive endorsement, making the sūtra a foundational scriptural authority for Advaita non-difference. *Bhedābheda* and Viśiṣṭādvaita commentators read *avasthitiḥ* less strongly — as Brahman's being-present-as-controller, not as identity. Beyond this single locus and its commentarial afterlife nothing of Kāśakṛtsna survives.
